Wayne Mazarire

Open-Source Tools · Procedural Art · Interactive Worlds

Proof of Work

Work Gallery

Real project outputs — 3D technical visualisations, procedural geometry, layered geological renders, and professional CAD environments.

⊕ View
Parametric Surface Visualisation
3D Modelling Procedural Geometry

Gradient-mapped parametric mesh surface with spiral mechanical sub-assembly. Demonstrates procedural mesh generation, material colour mapping, and complex scene composition on a black-void presentation background.

⊕ View
Terrain Mesh & Data Overlay
Surface Modelling Data Visualisation

Irregularly tessellated terrain surface with embedded structural rig and an in-scene bar chart overlay displaying volumetric data. Shows integration of quantitative data into a 3D visual context.

⊕ View
Layered Geological Section — Annotated
Technical Annotation Layer Compositing

Multi-strata geological render with fully dimensioned cross-section annotations (5.50m intervals), transparent layer compositing, and labelled ore domain regions. Precision spatial documentation work.

⊕ View
Open Pit Design — CAD Environment
CAD Modelling Technical Pipeline

Professional CAD workspace showing bench solids, structured layer hierarchy, and a fully populated attribute panel with cost, revenue, tonnage, and density properties. Complex multi-object scene management.

Selected Work

Asset Projects

PROJECT — 01
Blender 3D Pipeline 3D Render

Large-Scale Synthetic Object Render Library — 12,000 Images

Designed and rendered a 12,000-image library of household objects with randomised lighting (HDRI + procedural), material variation, camera angles, and occlusion. Geometry nodes drove object placement for full automation.

→ Delivered structured asset bundles with Python-generated metadata. Reduced manual processing overhead by ~80% for downstream production teams.
PROJECT — 02
Godot 4 GDScript Scene Design

Procedural Scene Engine for Interactive Environment Design

Built a Godot 4 environment generator that spawns randomised indoor scenes — varying furniture layout, surface textures, ambient light colour, and camera positions. Scripted bulk render export with paired depth and segmentation maps.

→ Generated 50,000+ structured image sets used across simulation and visualisation projects.
PROJECT — 03
LibreSprite GIMP Sprite Art

Pixel-Art Sprite Library — 200+ Animated Characters

Created a hand-crafted sprite library of 200+ animated characters across 8 palette variants and 4 animation states each. GIMP Script-Fu automated sheet slicing and metadata JSON generation.

→ Delivered a complete, production-ready sprite pack; assets integrated directly into a live interactive project with zero rework required.
PROJECT — 04
Inkscape Krita Annotation

Vector Annotation Toolkit for Structured Visual Workflows

Developed a reusable Inkscape + Krita workflow for overlaying segmentation masks and bounding-box annotations onto technical image exports. Produced SVG layers convertible directly to structured XML formats.

→ Toolkit adopted by a 4-person production team; cut per-image processing time from 8 min to under 2 min.
PROJECT — 05
Blender Krita Character Art

Rigged Character Pack — 15 Humanoid Characters, 2,500 Poses

Modelled, rigged, and textured 15 anatomically varied humanoid characters in Blender. Krita provided hand-painted skin and clothing textures. Delivered FBX and glTF exports with 2,500 keyframed poses spanning daily activities.

→ Full character pack delivered on schedule; immediately integrated into a real-time interactive experience with no geometry rework needed.
PROJECT — 06
Full Stack All Tools Pipeline

End-to-End Asset Pipeline for a Multi-Discipline Production Team

Architected a 6-tool pipeline: Inkscape concept sketches → Krita texture painting → Blender 3D integration → Godot scene assembly → GIMP post-processing → LibreSprite 2D derivative assets. Documented in full for team handoff.

→ Pipeline now supports a 3-person team generating 1,000+ assets/week with full version control and metadata tagging.

Background

About Wayne

I'm a digital asset specialist with a multidisciplinary background spanning 3D visualisation, 2D illustration, game-engine scripting, and production pipeline engineering. My work sits at the intersection of creative craft and technical systems.

Having built assets for interactive experiences, simulation projects, and large-scale visual productions, I understand what a project actually needs from its assets — not just what looks good on screen. I bring that pipeline-awareness to every asset I create.

I'm drawn to the challenge of scale: designing asset creation workflows that are systematic enough to produce thousands of consistent, well-structured outputs, while maintaining the craft quality that keeps production standards high.

All tools in my stack are open-source. I've contributed to community scripts, documented internal pipelines, and prefer solutions that are transparent, reproducible, and easy to hand off.
